A.rationale: Correct and richest (cites listobject.c, NaN/bool traps), but 2895 chars is verbose for a one-line question; signal-per-token weaker.

B.rationale: Corrects premise cleanly, complete-but-tight: position vs value, linear scan, ValueError, dict/set alternative. Best signal density.

C.rationale: Tightest (1513). Correct, good table including x-in-lst and dict/set, notes dict staleness on mutation. Slightly thinner on ValueError handling than B.

notes: All correct, safe, no tool use (pure question). B and C edge out A on conciseness; B chosen for completeness (ValueError handling, fix guidance) at tight length. A over-elaborates for a one-liner.
